facebook.content_angle: Stroke rate vs stroke length — the trade-off most club swimmers never consciously think about, and how finding your optimal combination unlocks faster times without extra fitness
facebook.post_text:
Stroke rate or stroke length — most swimmers never ask which one is actually costing them. 🎯

You're putting in the laps. Your fitness is there. But your times have plateaued — and you don't know why.

👉 Stroke rate (tempo) drives speed in sprints — but cranking it up without power just burns energy fast.
👉 Stroke length (distance per stroke) is efficiency — but too slow a tempo lets the water win.
👉 The sweet spot is different for every swimmer, and it shifts across distances.

The fastest swimmers in the world aren't the ones pulling the hardest or turning over the fastest — they're the ones who've found their optimal combination. 🔥

✔️ Count your strokes per length in training — track it weekly.
✔️ Try 10x25m: alternate high-tempo short strokes vs long slow strokes, then find the middle.
✔️ In race conditions, your optimal tempo will be slightly higher than training — plan for it.

This is the metric most club coaches mention once and most swimmers forget entirely. Don't be that swimmer.
facebook.engagement_bait: Do you naturally swim high-tempo or long and slow? Drop your stroke count per 25m below 👇
facebook.call_to_action: Do you know your stroke count per length — and have you ever actually trained to change it?
